variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
I've got thin evidence for a full beginner guide—your docs focus on barrier repair and mistakes, not a complete product table or timeline. I'll build what you asked for using best-effort structure and what's in the evidence, then flag what's missing. **core principle from your protocol:** barrier first, one active at a time, SPF non-negotiable.

don't stack acids + retinoids + vitamin c. most beginners fail on system design, not product choice.     **what's missing from your docs:** specific brand recommendations for cleansers, exact moisturizer names, and SPF product links. the evidence gives you the system (barrier repair + one active + SPF), not a full product catalog.

if you want specific product names or need to swap for what you already own, ask and I'll map it. **red flags to avoid:** don't exfoliate inflamed skin, don't skip SPF, don't stack actives. if your skin stings or flakes in week 1–2, pause the active and repair the barrier with ceramides + panthenol for 1–2 weeks before trying again.

products.name: La Roche-Posay Anthelios Melt-in Milk SPF 50+
products.description: High-protection broad-spectrum SPF 50 with cosmetic-elegant feel.

products.name: Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
products.description: OTC retinoid (adapalene 0.1%) — first-line for comedonal acne.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

steps.label: week 1–2: establish baseline
steps.detail: AM: cleanse + moisturize + SPF. PM: cleanse + moisturize.  no actives yet. goal: stable, non-reactive skin.

steps.label: week 3–4: introduce one active
steps.detail: add adapalene 0.1% or azelaic 20% 2–3x/week PM only.  watch for irritation (redness, stinging, dryness). if barrier reacts, pause and repair.

steps.label: week 5–6: tolerance build
steps.detail: increase active frequency to 4–5x/week if no irritation.  skin should feel less reactive, texture smoother.

steps.label: week 7–8: assess and adjust
steps.detail: if clear and stable, maintain routine. if still irritated, drop back to 2–3x/week.  do not stack a second active yet.

items: choose one gentle cleanser (CeraVe, cetaphil, or La Roche-Posay)
buy a ceramide + panthenol moisturizer (CeraVe moisturizing cream or cetaphil rich hydrating night cream)
get SPF 30+ (EltaMD UV clear or La Roche-Posay anthelios)
pick ONE active: adapalene 0.1% (differin) OR azelaic 20% (The Ordinary or finacea)
start with 2–3x/week active application, PM only
pause exfoliating acids, scrubs, and vitamin c until barrier is stable
track irritation signs: redness, stinging, dryness, flaking
after 4 weeks stable, consider increasing active frequency to 4–5x/week

cards.label: barrier stabilization window
cards.hint: no actives; cleanse + moisturize + SPF only

cards.label: starting active frequency
cards.hint: adapalene or azelaic; increase only if no irritation

cards.label: maximum rule for beginners
cards.hint: never stack acids + retinoids + vitamin c
